what_it_does: MCP tool wrapper that exposes image operations (store, read, list, versions, comments, notes, replies, threading, linking) for image://<channel>/<path> artifacts, delegating to runtime cc_images, cc_board, and cc_attachments.
observations: Registers one MCP tool via @mcp.tool() that dispatches on an op parameter.
Uses hierarchical addressing image://<channel>/<path> and attaches stored images to a channel.
Supports both base64 data and local file paths as image input.
Implements versioning, threaded comments/replies via board edges, and cross-reference links.
Reuses runtime modules rather than creating parallel storage machinery.
Docstring lists a smaller set of ops than the OPS tuple and implemented code (versions/note/reply/thread are present but not in the docstring ops list).
standouts: Hierarchical image:// channel/path addressing with versioning.
Threaded annotation tree built from commented_on and reply_to board edges.
Cross-reference linking via a references edge between two artifacts.
File-drop style tool that auto-registers on MCP server start.
conventions: Imports runtime modules inside the tool function rather than at module top.
Uses Literal string union for the op parameter.
Default empty strings for optional string arguments.
Closures for helpers (_serve, _thread, nest) scoped inside the tool function.
Board items carry links as kind/target pairs.
concerns: Docstring ops list and OPS tuple differ; versions/note/reply/thread are implemented but not documented in the ops section.
link op falls back to a hardcoded author_session string when none is supplied.
comment/note/reply ops do not validate that the target image or comment exists before filing.
list prefix construction mixes channel and path semantics; groups are only returned when path is empty.
get uses ci.get_image(image) directly without normalizing the image:// prefix or version suffix.
notes: A thin MCP facade that turns images into addressable, versioned, comment-threaded fabric artifacts by wiring together existing runtime seams.
questions: What is the expected format for version suffixes (e.g., @v<n>) and how does ci.get_image parse them?
Does ci.list_images interpret the prefix as a literal path prefix or as a channel/path hierarchy?
Is the hardcoded author_session fallback in link op intentional for anonymous links?
Are note and reply intended to be public ops, or internal helpers exposed by accident?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly aligned: the file stores, reads, lists, comments on, and links images as described, but it also implements versions/note/reply/thread ops that the module docstring's ops list omits.
apparent_themes: Reuse existing seams over new machinery
intention_signals: To give external agents and reviewers a single MCP surface for depositing images, comparing generated outputs against sources, and carrying threaded conversations anchored to specific image addresses.
novelty: Combines hierarchical image:// addressing, versioning, threaded board-edge annotations, and cross-references in one auto-registered MCP tool rather than separate tools.
contribution: An MCP tool binding that exposes image storage, retrieval, versioning, listing, threaded comments/notes/replies, and artifact linking while delegating all persistence and graph operations to existing runtime modules.
summary_for_embedding: MCP tool wrapper exposing image:// storage, versioning, listing, threaded annotations, and cross-references by delegating to runtime image, board, and attachment modules.
intention_for_embedding: Provide an agent-facing surface for images as first-class, reviewable, linkable fabric artifacts.

symbols.does: Registers the cc_images tool with the MCP server.
symbols.description: Decorated with @mcp.tool(), it binds the cc_images function into the MCP surface and prepares the runtime imports and store reference from suite.

symbols.does: MCP tool entrypoint that dispatches image operations.
symbols.description: Accepts an op and related parameters, validates required fields, and routes to runtime cc_images, cc_board, and cc_attachments helpers for storage, retrieval, versioning, comments, replies, threading, linking, and listing.

symbols.does: Builds a local HTTP serve URL for an image address.
symbols.description: Strips any image:// scheme from the address and returns /api/image/{id} so clients can fetch the stored image bytes.

symbols.does: Returns a nested annotation thread for a given address.
symbols.description: Uses reverse_traverse on commented_on edges to collect top-level comments and notes, then recursively collects reply_to edges via the inner nest helper to build a reply tree.

symbols.does: Recursively gathers replies to a comment or note.
symbols.description: Called by _thread to build nested reply trees by following reply_to edges from a given board item address.
